concepts: compression release with retained intraday value

thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. Live context says the only positive walk-forward cluster is still ETH/1h channel-shortbias, but that lane is now over-concentrated across family, asset, timeframe, and concept, and the latest same-family exploit refinements already degraded badly (one zero-trade screen fail and one six-trade collapse). Current completed failures also cluster in SOL/4h and BTC/1h event-driven structures. Rotate away from all of that while staying inside the supported universe. Target TAO 1h and demand 3 materially different but density-first intraday specs: one broad trend-state pullback continuation branch that stays active while price accepts above a fast/medium anchor stack, one failed-breakdown reclaim branch that turns on only after downside inventory is reclaimed and expansion starts, and one compression-release branch that enters only if intraday value is retained after expansion rather than requiring ceremonial breakout stacking. Do not clone ETH channel, ETH KAMA-stoch, ETH tail-harvester, or the recent BTC/SOL failed-breakdown grammar, and do not write narrow event stacks that cannot plausibly clear the 15-trade floor.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 15, and max_drawdown_pct <= 9.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.00, or max_drawdown_pct > 12.0.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: ETH/1h channel-shortbias now dominates the live scored evidence and recent same-family exploit refinements degraded, so TAO/1h is the clean supported rotation away from the over-concentrated ETH lane and the recent SOL/BTC failure cluster.
